nodes.goal: Load Back Bay OSM layers (surface, parks, water, roads, buildings) from a local PBF.
Fan the loaded layers out to the baseline and modified shadow-compute branches so the PBF is parsed once.

nodes.content:
{
  "compute": [
    {
      "dataRef": "table_osm_roads",
      "attributes": {
        "seg": "geometry.coordinates"
      },
      "attributeMatrices": {
        "seg": {
          "rows": "auto",
          "cols": 2
        }
      },
      "uniforms": {
        "bld_height": {
          "fromFeature": {
            "layer": "table_osm_buildings",
            "iterate": "batched",
            "path": "properties.height",
            "required": true
          }
        },
        "doy": 172
      },
      "uniformMatrices": {
        "ring": {
          "fromFeature": {
            "layer": "table_osm_buildings",
            "iterate": "batched",
            "path": "geometry.coordinates.0"
          },
          "cols": 2
        }
      },
      "outputColumnName": "sunlight",
      "wglsFunction": [
        "let pi      = 3.14159265359;",
        "let lat_rad = 0.7393;",
        "let lon_ref = -75.0;",
        "let lon_loc = -71.06;",
        "",
        "let dec_rad = -0.40928 * cos(2.0 * pi / 365.0 * (doy + 10.0));",
        "",
        "let ax = seg[0u]; let ay = seg[1u];",
        "let bx = seg[2u]; let by = seg[3u];",
        "",
        "let n_buildings: u32 = u32(num_features);",
        "",
        "var sunlight: f32 = 0.0;",
        "for (var hour = 7i; hour <= 19i; hour++) {",
        "    let solar_time = f32(hour) + (lon_ref - lon_loc) / 15.0;",
        "    let h_rad = (solar_time - 12.0) * pi / 12.0;",
        "    let sin_alt = sin(lat_rad) * sin(dec_rad) + cos(lat_rad) * cos(dec_rad) * cos(h_rad);",
        "    let alt_rad = asin(clamp(sin_alt, -1.0, 1.0));",
        "    if alt_rad <= 0.0 { continue; }",
        "",
        "    let az_rad = atan2(sin(h_rad), sin(lat_rad) * cos(h_rad) - cos(lat_rad) * tan(dec_rad)) + pi;",
        "    let sdx = -sin(az_rad);",
        "    let sdy = -cos(az_rad);",
        "    let perp_x = sdy;",
        "    let perp_y = -sdx;",
        "",
        "    let au = ax * perp_x + ay * perp_y;",
        "    let av = ax * sdx + ay * sdy;",
        "    let bu = bx * perp_x + by * perp_y;",
        "    let bv = bx * sdx + by * sdy;",
        "",
        "    var any_hit = false;",
        "    for (var bi = 0u; bi < n_buildings; bi++) {",
        "        let height = bld_height[bi];",
        "        let shadow_len = height / tan(alt_rad);",
        "        let base = bi * 8u;",
        "",
        "        var u_lo: f32 =  1e30; var u_hi: f32 = -1e30;",
        "        var v_lo: f32 =  1e30; var v_hi: f32 = -1e30;",
        "        for (var ci = 0u; ci < 4u; ci++) {",
        "            let rx = ring[base + ci * 2u];",
        "            let ry = ring[base + ci * 2u + 1u];",
        "            let ru = rx * perp_x + ry * perp_y;",
        "            let rv = rx * sdx + ry * sdy;",
        "            u_lo = min(u_lo, ru); u_hi = max(u_hi, ru);",
        "            v_lo = min(v_lo, rv); v_hi = max(v_hi, rv);",
        "        }",
        "        let v_hi_shadow = v_hi + shadow_len;",
        "",
        "        var hit = false;",
        "        if au >= u_lo && au <= u_hi && av >= v_lo && av <= v_hi_shadow { hit = true; }",
        "        else if bu >= u_lo && bu <= u_hi && bv >= v_lo && bv <= v_hi_shadow { hit = true; }",
        "        else if !(max(au, bu) < u_lo || min(au, bu) > u_hi) &&",
        "                !(max(av, bv) < v_lo || min(av, bv) > v_hi_shadow) {",
        "            let du = bu - au;",
        "            let dv = bv - av;",
        "            if abs(du) > 0.001 {",
        "                let t0 = (u_lo - au) / du;",
        "                let t1 = (u_hi - au) / du;",
        "                let t_enter = max(min(t0, t1), 0.0);",
        "                let t_exit  = min(max(t0, t1), 1.0);",
        "                if t_enter <= t_exit {",
        "                    let v_enter = av + t_enter * dv;",
        "                    let v_exit  = av + t_exit  * dv;",
        "                    if max(v_enter, v_exit) >= v_lo && min(v_enter, v_exit) <= v_hi_shadow {",
        "                        hit = true;",
        "                    }",
        "                }",
        "            } else {",
        "                if max(av, bv) >= v_lo && min(av, bv) <= v_hi_shadow { hit = true; }",
        "            }",
        "        }",
        "        if hit { any_hit = true; break; }",
        "    }",
        "",
        "    if !any_hit { sunlight += 60.0; }",
        "}",
        "",
        "return sunlight;"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
nodes.goal: Baseline shadow study — per road segment, accumulate minutes of June-solstice sunlight using each Back Bay building's REAL OSM height as the shadow caster.
Baseline render — roads coloured by minutes of sunlight under real building heights.

nodes.content:
{
  "compute": [
    {
      "dataRef": "table_osm_roads",
      "attributes": {
        "seg": "geometry.coordinates"
      },
      "attributeMatrices": {
        "seg": {
          "rows": "auto",
          "cols": 2
        }
      },
      "uniforms": {
        "bld_height": {
          "fromFeature": {
            "layer": "table_osm_buildings",
            "iterate": "batched",
            "path": "properties.height",
            "required": true
          }
        },
        "doy": 172
      },
      "uniformMatrices": {
        "ring": {
          "fromFeature": {
            "layer": "table_osm_buildings",
            "iterate": "batched",
            "path": "geometry.coordinates.0"
          },
          "cols": 2
        }
      },
      "outputColumnName": "sunlight",
      "wglsFunction": [
        "let pi      = 3.14159265359;",
        "let lat_rad = 0.7393;",
        "let lon_ref = -75.0;",
        "let lon_loc = -71.06;",
        "",
        "let dec_rad = -0.40928 * cos(2.0 * pi / 365.0 * (doy + 10.0));",
        "",
        "let ax = seg[0u]; let ay = seg[1u];",
        "let bx = seg[2u]; let by = seg[3u];",
        "",
        "let n_buildings: u32 = u32(num_features);",
        "",
        "var sunlight: f32 = 0.0;",
        "for (var hour = 7i; hour <= 19i; hour++) {",
        "    let solar_time = f32(hour) + (lon_ref - lon_loc) / 15.0;",
        "    let h_rad = (solar_time - 12.0) * pi / 12.0;",
        "    let sin_alt = sin(lat_rad) * sin(dec_rad) + cos(lat_rad) * cos(dec_rad) * cos(h_rad);",
        "    let alt_rad = asin(clamp(sin_alt, -1.0, 1.0));",
        "    if alt_rad <= 0.0 { continue; }",
        "",
        "    let az_rad = atan2(sin(h_rad), sin(lat_rad) * cos(h_rad) - cos(lat_rad) * tan(dec_rad)) + pi;",
        "    let sdx = -sin(az_rad);",
        "    let sdy = -cos(az_rad);",
        "    let perp_x = sdy;",
        "    let perp_y = -sdx;",
        "",
        "    let au = ax * perp_x + ay * perp_y;",
        "    let av = ax * sdx + ay * sdy;",
        "    let bu = bx * perp_x + by * perp_y;",
        "    let bv = bx * sdx + by * sdy;",
        "",
        "    var any_hit = false;",
        "    for (var bi = 0u; bi < n_buildings; bi++) {",
        "        // What-if: every building is 2x taller, so its shadow doubles too.",
        "        let height = 2.0 * bld_height[bi];",
        "        let shadow_len = height / tan(alt_rad);",
        "        let base = bi * 8u;",
        "",
        "        var u_lo: f32 =  1e30; var u_hi: f32 = -1e30;",
        "        var v_lo: f32 =  1e30; var v_hi: f32 = -1e30;",
        "        for (var ci = 0u; ci < 4u; ci++) {",
        "            let rx = ring[base + ci * 2u];",
        "            let ry = ring[base + ci * 2u + 1u];",
        "            let ru = rx * perp_x + ry * perp_y;",
        "            let rv = rx * sdx + ry * sdy;",
        "            u_lo = min(u_lo, ru); u_hi = max(u_hi, ru);",
        "            v_lo = min(v_lo, rv); v_hi = max(v_hi, rv);",
        "        }",
        "        let v_hi_shadow = v_hi + shadow_len;",
        "",
        "        var hit = false;",
        "        if au >= u_lo && au <= u_hi && av >= v_lo && av <= v_hi_shadow { hit = true; }",
        "        else if bu >= u_lo && bu <= u_hi && bv >= v_lo && bv <= v_hi_shadow { hit = true; }",
        "        else if !(max(au, bu) < u_lo || min(au, bu) > u_hi) &&",
        "                !(max(av, bv) < v_lo || min(av, bv) > v_hi_shadow) {",
        "            let du = bu - au;",
        "            let dv = bv - av;",
        "            if abs(du) > 0.001 {",
        "                let t0 = (u_lo - au) / du;",
        "                let t1 = (u_hi - au) / du;",
        "                let t_enter = max(min(t0, t1), 0.0);",
        "                let t_exit  = min(max(t0, t1), 1.0);",
        "                if t_enter <= t_exit {",
        "                    let v_enter = av + t_enter * dv;",
        "                    let v_exit  = av + t_exit  * dv;",
        "                    if max(v_enter, v_exit) >= v_lo && min(v_enter, v_exit) <= v_hi_shadow {",
        "                        hit = true;",
        "                    }",
        "                }",
        "            } else {",
        "                if max(av, bv) >= v_lo && min(av, bv) <= v_hi_shadow { hit = true; }",
        "            }",
        "        }",
        "        if hit { any_hit = true; break; }",
        "    }",
        "",
        "    if !any_hit { sunlight += 60.0; }",
        "}",
        "",
        "return sunlight;"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
nodes.goal: What-if shadow study — same shader as the baseline but every building's height is doubled inside the shadow loop, so shadows extend 2x and roads collect less sunlight near tall structures.
Modified render — same map layout as the baseline; roads coloured by the doubled-height sunlight result, side-by-side for visual comparison.

dataflow.name: Autark what-if shadow study
dataflow.description: What-if shadow study for Boston's Back Bay built on Example 7's per-road sunlight shader. A shared data node loads the PBF once and a data-pool fans the layers out to two parallel branches: a baseline branch runs the shadow shader with each building's real OSM height, and a modified branch runs the same shader but doubles every building's height inside the shadow loop. Two side-by-side maps render roads coloured by accumulated June-solstice sunlight, so the visual diff is the loss of road sunlight when every building gets twice as tall.
